fields.label: Metabase Base URL
fields.helpText: Your Metabase instance root URL. For self-hosted: https://metabase.example.com. For Metabase Cloud: https://your-tenant.metabaseapp.com. Do NOT include /api at the end.

fields.label: API Key
fields.helpText: Admin Settings -> Authentication -> API Keys -> Create API Key. Requires Pro/Enterprise or self-hosted instance.

healthcheck_config.description: Validates API Key via current-user endpoint

metadata.summary: Metabase open-source BI -- execute saved questions (cards), read dashboards, and manage alerts/pulses across your connected databases.
metadata.setup_guide:
1. Sign in to Metabase as an admin
2. Go to Admin Settings -> Authentication -> API Keys
3. Click 'Create API Key', name it, and choose the group whose permissions it should inherit
4. Copy the mb_... key (shown only once)
5. Paste your base URL (e.g. https://metabase.example.com) and the API key here

Note: API keys require Metabase Pro, Enterprise, or a self-hosted instance.

llm_usage_hint.overview: Metabase REST API. Auth header: 'X-API-Key: <api_key>' (case-insensitive). Base URL is user-supplied; all endpoints under /api. Core resources: card (saved questions), dashboard, alert, pulse, database, collection.
gotchas: Auth header is 'X-API-Key', NOT 'Authorization'. Do not prefix with Bearer or Token.
POST /api/card/<id>/query returns results synchronously (rows + cols). Use /query/csv or /query/json for pre-formatted exports.
'parameters' in the query body is an ARRAY of {type, target, value} shapes, unlike Redash's object. Read the card definition first to discover parameter targets.
API keys inherit the permissions of the group they were created under -- scope them to read-only groups for agent consumption.
Base URL must not include trailing /api. The healthcheck appends /api/user/current itself.
